description_long: This chaise features a minimalist, geometric design with a solid, angular frame and a low-profile silhouette. The backrest is slightly elevated in the center, creating a subtle peak, and extends across the full width of the seat, providing support for two people. Both sides of the chaise are flanked by short, integrated armrests that blend seamlessly into the overall structure. The seat is broad and flat, with a central dividing line suggesting a dual seating arrangement. The legs are short, blocky, and set at the corners, giving the piece a sturdy and stable appearance. The surface is uniformly smooth and matte, with no visible upholstery pattern or decorative elements.
description: A modern, angular chaise with a solid, smooth surface, integrated armrests, and a central backrest peak. The design is minimalist and sturdy, suitable for urban outdoor settings.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full width of the chaise, the central peak in the backrest, and both integrated armrests.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the angular profile, the short blocky leg, and the sloped backrest.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the flat, unadorned backrest and the overall rectangular shape.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirrors view 1, showing the same angular profile and leg structure.
attribute_backrest_design: The backrest is solid, slightly elevated in the center with a gentle peak, and extends the full width of the chaise. It is flat and unadorned, with no visible decorative elements or segmentation.
attribute_armrest_presence: Armrests are present on both sides, integrated into the main structure with a low, angular profile.
attribute_leg_style: The legs are short, blocky, and rectangular, positioned at the corners and constructed from the same material as the rest of the chaise, with no decorative features.
attribute_upholstery_pattern: There is no visible upholstery; the surface is smooth, solid, and untextured, with a matte finish.
attribute_cushion_arrangement: No separate cushions are visible; the seat and backrest are formed from a single, continuous material with a central dividing line suggesting dual seating.
